data.description:
Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) is back in focus after TIME named it among 2026’s 10 Most Influential Health & Life Science Companies, alongside encouraging Phase 1/2 BEACON data in sickle cell disease treatment.

See our latest analysis for Beam Therapeutics.

Investors appear to be responding to the TIME recognition and encouraging BEACON data, with the 7-day share price return of 13.24% and 30-day share price return of 26.75% pointing to strengthening momentum. The 1-year total shareholder return of 51.68% contrasts with weaker 3 and 5 year total shareholder returns.

If Beam’s recent run has your attention, it could be worth seeing what else is moving in high growth health and biotech, starting with 33 healthcare AI stocks.

With Beam now valued at around US$3.0b and recent returns sharply ahead of its 3 and 5 year record, the key question is whether the current price still lags its long term potential or already reflects future growth.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 79.5% Undervalued

Compared with Beam’s last close at $30.70, the most widely followed narrative assigns a fair value of $150.00 per share, pointing to a very wide gap between price and perceived potential.

The "Molecular Pencil": Why Beam's Technology is Built to Win The investment thesis in Beam Therapeutics is a long-term, high-conviction bet on a fundamental technological shift in genetic medicine. While first-generation gene editors like CRISPR-Cas9 function as "molecular scissors," they are an inherently disruptive tool. By creating double-strand DNA breaks, they introduce significant risks, including unpredictable insertions, deletions, and large genomic rearrangements. Beam represents "Gene Editing 2.0." Its base editing platform is a "molecular pencil," a precision instrument that chemically rewrites a single "letter" of the genetic code without cutting the DNA backbone. This approach offers a potentially superior safety and precision profile, designed to mitigate the core risks that plague its predecessors. This thesis is no longer just theoretical. Beam achieved a landmark milestone with BEAM-302, providing the first-ever clinical proof-of-concept for in vivo base editing in humans. This "one-and-done" therapy for AATD directly corrects the disease-causing PiZ mutation. Early data is exceptionally strong, showing it achieves the dual therapeutic goal: it halts the production of the toxic liver protein (Z-AAT) while simultaneously restoring the functional, protective lung protein (M-AAT). This establishes a clear best-in-class profile and significantly de-risks the entire liver-directed LNP platform.

Narratives take the story you believe about FactSet Research Systems, tie it to your own revenue, earnings and margin estimates, then link that to a Fair Value that you can compare with the current share price to help decide whether the gap looks attractive or stretched.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an easy tool that lets you set this story in a few inputs. The platform then connects your view to a live valuation that updates automatically when new information such as earnings, acquisitions or AI product news is added.

For FactSet Research Systems, one investor might focus on its role as a financial infrastructure provider with products like Cobalt, CUSIP, Vermilion and Portware and arrive at a Fair Value around US$313.99. Another might lean on a more cautious analyst style view and land closer to US$252.44. Both are simply expressing different Narratives that the platform can track and refresh as the facts change.
